description_long: This clock tower is constructed primarily from weathered wood, giving it a rustic and historic appearance. The structure is tall and narrow, featuring multiple stacked sections with protruding ledges and decorative trim. Several dark, glass-like window panels are set into the tower at different heights, with the uppermost section capped by a steep, triangular gabled roof. The facade is adorned with geometric wooden accents and cross-bracing, adding to its architectural interest. The overall design suggests a handcrafted, old-world style, possibly inspired by medieval or early European town architecture. No visible clock faces are present on any side of the tower.
description: A tall, narrow wooden clock tower with a steep gabled roof, dark window panels, and rustic, geometric detailing. The structure has a weathered, historic look with no visible clock faces.
description_view_0: Side view showing stacked wooden sections, three window panels, and a protruding ledge near the top.
description_view_1: Front view with a prominent triangular gabled roof, cross-braced wooden accents, and a central window panel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with a single large window and visible wooden paneling, less ornate than the front.
description_view_3: Rear view featuring a gabled roof, a single window, and cross-bracing details similar to the front but with less ornamentation.
